title: Stool collection device and stool sampling device
abstract: A stool collection device (10) comprising a rigid dissolvable fibre sheet adapted to fit above the water in a toilet pan.
claims: Claims ( 25 ) A stool collection device comprising a dissolvable sheet adapted to fit above the water in a toilet pan. A stool collection device according to Claim 1, in which the sheet has one or two holes in the centre part. A stool collection device according to Claim 1, in which the sheet has a cut in the sheet in order to enable a tab to be pushed out in a central portion of the sheet. A stool collection device according to any preceding Claim, in which the sheet has a V- or U-shaped cut out is provided in one side of the sheet. A stool collection device according to any preceding Claim, in which the corners of the sheet are curved. A stool collection device according to Claim 6, in which the corners can have close to them serrations cut out in order to enable the sheet to shape. A stool collection device according to any preceding Claim, in which the sheet is designed to fit in an envelope, preferably a C5 envelope. A stool collection device according to any preceding Claim, in which the sheet comprises sprayed paper fibre, dissolvable paper, dissolvable cellulose, papier mÃ¢chÃ©, degradable pulp board, dissolvable fabric and sheets created from the spraying of paper pulp onto a wire mesh shape or combinations thereof. A stool collection device according to any preceding Claim, in which the thickness of the sheet is between 2 and 3 mm. A stool collection device according to any preceding Claim, in which the stool sampler is constructed such that it has two halves which are mirror images or inverted mirror images of each other. A stool collection device according to Claim 10, in which the join of the two halves has a pre-scored bendable axis. A stool collection device according to any preceding Claim, in which the stool sampler has four incisions cut, one in each corner. A stool collection device according to any one of Claims 1 to 11, in which the shape of the sampler is substantially circular with four incisions. A stool collection device according to any one of Claims 1 to 11, in which the shape of the sheet is substantially circular or circular with two flattened edges with at least two incisions pre-scored or cut. A stool collection device according to any one of Claims 1 to 11, in which the shape of the sheet is rectangular with circular ends with at least two incisions. A stool collection device according to Claim 14 or Claim 15, in which incisions can either be straight cuts or cut out segments. A stool collection device according to any one of Claims 14 to 16, in which there are four incisions. A stool collection device according to Claim 17, in which the incisions are such that they cut out segments roughly at right angles to each other. A stool collection device according to any one of Claims 14 or claims 16 to 18 read appendant to Claim 14, in which at least two incisions are in the middle of each flattened edge with the pre-scored axis between them A stool collection device according to any one of Claims 15 or claims 16 to 18 read appendant to Claim 15, in which two incisions are in the middle of each curved end. A stool collection device according to any one of Claims 14 to 20, in which at least two incisions are equipped with joining means. A stool collection device according to Claim 21, in which the joining means are a hook like protrusion in the incision or a semi-circular protrusion pre-scored next to the incision and an accepting cut out next to the incision, preferably a hole, A stool collection device according to Claim 22, in which the hole has a half moon shape. A stool collection device according to Claim 23, in which the half-moon shaped hole is approximately twice the size of the protrusion. A stool collection device according to any preceding claim, in which the edge of the sampler comprises castellations.
